channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Call for urgent probe into irregular issuance of Kenyan passports and illegal recruitment
item.description: The Kenyan government is facing serious allegations over the illegal sale of passports to foreigners and the secret recruitment of jobless youths to fight in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. Opposition le...

item.title: Kasasa Island residents concerned over Chinese land purchases amid Japan-China geopolitical strains
item.description: Kasasa Island, a small, largely abandoned island in Japan's Seto Inland Sea with only seven registered residents, has become a focal point for growing tensions between Japan and China. The island, kno...

item.title: Lessons on Time and Discipline from National Artist Rio Alma
item.description: National Artist for Literature Virgilio S. Almario, also known as Rio Alma, exemplifies the value of time both in personal discipline and cultural wisdom. Known for always arriving early to classes, m...

item.title: Nigeria Power Generation Falls Below 4,000MW, Causing Extended Blackouts
item.description: Nigeria don waka enter serious wahala with electricity as generation don drop below 4,000 megawatts again, this time to 3,940MW due to gas shortage wey dey affect thermal power plants nationwide. The ...

item.title: Iran Appoints Mojtaba Khamenei As Supreme Leader Following Father’s Death
item.description: Iran don appoint Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader after him papa, Ali Khamenei, waka pass away, according to state media. The Assembly of Experts, wey get power to pick supreme leader, talk say ...

item.title: UK launches charter flights for British nationals stranded in Dubai amid Middle East conflict
item.description: The UK Foreign Office has launched an online booking portal for British nationals in Dubai who wish to access government-operated charter flights out of the Middle East. This follows widespread flight...

item.title: Amihan and Low Pressure Area expected to cause widespread rain in the Philippines
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has forecasted increased rainfall across several regions of the country due to the northeast monsoon, locally ...

item.title: Australian shares fall sharply amid rising oil prices due to Iran conflict
item.description: The Australian share market suffered a significant fall on Monday, dropping 3.4 per cent and wiping more than $100 billion off market value. This decline was driven by a sharp rise in oil prices follo...

item.title: India confirms no Indian casualties in Al-Kharj projectile incident in Saudi Arabia
item.description: On 8th March 2026, a projectile landed in a residential area in Al-Kharj city, Saudi Arabia, causing tragic fatalities. Initial reports suggested that among the deceased were an Indian and a Banglades...

item.title: Prison Inmate Claims Mismanagement Around Jeffrey Epstein’s Death
item.description: A fresh account from a prisoner who was held at the Metropolitan Correctional Centre in New York has raised renewed questions about the death of Jeffrey Epstein, which authorities ruled as suicide. Th...

item.title: Iran accuses US and Israel of war crimes over airstrikes hitting civilian sites and infrastructure
item.description: According to a report in Dawn newspaper, the United States and Israel are facing serious accusations of committing war crimes in Iran due to recent airstrikes that have caused heavy civilian casualtie...

item.title: Vancouver delays installation of suicide-prevention fencing on Granville Street Bridge due to budget limits
item.description: City staff in Vancouver have recommended scaling back the planned installation of suicide-prevention fencing on the Granville Street Bridge, citing funding limitations as the primary obstacle. The pro...

item.title: Police Detain Woman After Shots Fired at Rihanna's Home in Beverly Hills
item.description: On Sunday afternoon, a woman armed with an AR-15 rifle fired several rounds into the home of pop star Rihanna in Beverly Hills, California. Law enforcement confirmed that one bullet penetrated the man...

item.title: Presidency Denies Alleged Poison Plot By Aso Rock Kitchen Staff
item.description: The Presidency don clarify say all those tori wey dey circulate for social media about kitchen staff for Aso Rock trying to poison President Bola Tinubu na pure false news. Bayo Onanuga, wey dey serve...

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene Criticizes White House Over Hypothetical Military Draft Remarks
item.description: Former Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene reacted strongly after White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt appeared on Fox News’ Sunday Morning Futures. During the interview, host Maria Bartiromo...

item.title: Rising Ethiopia-Eritrea Tensions Put Egypt in Difficult Strategic Position
item.description: Three years after Ethiopia and Eritrea fought together against the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) in the Tigray war, the two former allies are now moving closer to confrontation again. Addis ...

item.title: Peter Obi, Senators Attend ADC Membership Registration and Revalidation in Anambra
item.description: Peter Obi, former governor of Anambra State and 2023 Labour Party presidential candidate, join forces with Senators Victor Umeh and Tony Nwoye for the African Democratic Congress (ADC) membership regi...

item.title: Matildas Draw with South Korea, Face Tougher Path to Asian Cup Quarter-Finals
item.description: The Matildas secured a 3-3 draw against South Korea in their final Group A match at the Women's Asian Cup, but it was a result that complicated their route to the quarter-finals. Despite two earlier w...

item.title: Health Officials Say Trace Chemicals in Sanitary Pads Pose No Proven Harm
item.description: Health minister Aaron Motsoaledi has urged the public not to panic over recent findings of endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs) in certain sanitary pads and panty-liners sold in South Africa. During ...

item.title: Jane Mugoh Advises Single Mothers to Choose Supportive Partners on International Women’s Day
item.description: On International Women's Day, private detective Jane Mugoh shared her personal experiences and reflections on love and marriage, particularly addressing single mothers. Speaking to TUKO.co.ke, she rev...
